description: Examines aligned records in the supplied SAM or BAM file to locate duplicate reads.
author: Picard Tools
plan:
## Picard.MarkDuplicates GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
Picard.MarkDuplicates is a GenePattern module that Examines aligned records in the supplied SAM or BAM file to locate duplicate reads.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 7 parameters:
- Required: input.file, remove.duplicates, max.file.handles, optical.duplicate.distance, sorting.collection.size.ratio, output.prefix
- Optional: read.name.regex

### 3. Command Line
```
<java> <java_flags> -cp <libdir>MarkDuplicates.jar<path.separator><libdir>commons-cli-1.2.jar<path.separator><libdir>commons-lang-2.5.jar<path.separator><libdir>gp-MarkDuplicates.jar org.genepattern.sam.MarkDuplicatesWrapper -inputFileName <input.file> -removeDup <remove.duplicates> -maxFileH <max.file.handles> -rnRegex <read.name.regex> -dist <optical.duplicate.distance> -outputFileName <output.prefix> -ratio <sorting.collection.size.ratio>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: 

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: max.file.handles
parameters.description: Maximum number of file handles to keep open when spilling read ends to disk

parameters.name: read.name.regex
parameters.description: Regular expression that can be used to parse read names in the input file

parameters.name: optical.duplicate.distance
parameters.description: The maximum offset between two duplicate clusters in order to consider them optical duplicates. This should usually be set to some fairly small number (e.g. 5-10 pixels) unless using later versions of the Illumina pipeline that multiply pixel values by 10, in which case 50-100 is more normal

parameters.name: sorting.collection.size.ratio
parameters.description: This number, plus the maximum RAM available to the JVM, determine the memory footprint used by some of the sorting collections.  If you are running out of memory, try reducing this number

parameters.name: output.prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to use for the output file name
